commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Thus says the LORD unto me, Go and buy you a linen belt, and put it upon your waist, and put it not in water. Girdle, usually worn by women. Jerome) (Exodus xxviii. ) It denoted the Jews, who became more corrupt during the captivity; (ver. ) so that God delivered them out of his pure mercy. Thus He explains this prophetical action. (Calmet) Loins. God cherished his people. (Menochius) Water. He made choice of them when they were rough and deformed. Jerome) Their sins cause their ruin.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
